title: Essay on Shinylive
text:
Shinylive is a modern technology that allows Shiny applications to run directly inside a web browser without requiring a separate R server. It is designed to make deployment simpler and more accessible.
Shinylive uses WebAssembly technology to run R entirely in the browser, making it possible to create serverless Shiny applications.


Traditional Shiny applications require a server to process calculations and user interactions. In contrast, Shinylive allows the application to run completely on the client side inside the user’s browser.
This means users can interact with the application without connecting to a dedicated Shiny server.





Traditional Shiny apps:

Require server hosting
Depend on an active R session
Process calculations on the server
Often require deployment platforms such as Shiny Server or shinyapps.io




Shinylive applications:

Run directly in the browser
Do not require server hosting
Use WebAssembly technology
Are easier to deploy as static websites





Some major advantages include:

Easier deployment
No server maintenance
Lower hosting costs
Faster sharing of applications
Better portability for educational projects




Despite its benefits, Shinylive also has limitations:

Limited support for some R packages
Browser performance limitations
Less suitable for large-scale enterprise applications
Can struggle with very large datasets




Shinylive is especially useful for:

Educational demonstrations
Lightweight dashboards
Portfolio projects
Online tutorials
Interactive learning materials

Shinylive

title: Home
text:
Hi! My name is Bhamini Raghuram Pillai, and I am currently pursuing a Master’s degree in Digital Marketing at California State Polytechnic University, with an expected graduation year of 2026.
I am passionate about marketing analytics, data-driven decision making, and digital strategy. My goal is to build a career where I can combine business knowledge with analytics and technology to create impactful marketing solutions.


My career interests include:

Marketing Analytics
Digital Marketing Strategy
Business Intelligence
Data Visualization

Marketing Technology

title: Essay on Shiny Apps
text:
Shiny is an R package that allows users to build interactive web applications directly from R. It is widely used in data science, analytics, and business intelligence because it helps transform static analysis into interactive dashboards and applications.


A Shiny app is a web application built using the R programming language. It allows users to interact with data visualizations, dashboards, and analytical tools through a web browser.
Shiny applications are commonly used for:

Interactive dashboards
Data visualization
Business reporting
Statistical analysis
Marketing analytics
Research projects




A traditional Shiny app usually contains two major components:


The UI controls the appearance of the app. It includes:

Titles
Layouts
Charts
Buttons
Menus
Filters




The server controls the logic and calculations behind the application. It processes user inputs and generates outputs such as charts and tables.




Some major benefits of Shiny apps include:

Interactive user experience
Real-time data analysis
Easy integration with R
Strong visualization capabilities
Useful for business intelligence dashboards




Although Shiny is powerful, it also has some limitations:

Can require server hosting
Performance issues with large datasets
More advanced apps require stronger programming skills
Deployment can sometimes be complicated




Shiny apps are highly useful in marketing analytics because they allow businesses to monitor campaign performance, customer behavior, and website traffic interactively.
For example, companies can use Shiny apps to:

Track digital marketing campaigns
Analyze customer engagement
Visualize sales trends
Monitor conversion rates
